Items.Title: 你希望你有时间学习什么？ | What do you wish you had time to learn?
Items.Description:
本·哈尔彭

发表于一天只有这么多小时——您希望您可以为软件开发中的哪些工具和/或学科腾出时间？



原文:

Ben Halpern

Posted on Apr 27

There are only so many hours in the day — which tools and/or disciplines in software development do you wish you could make time for?
Items.Author: ben

Items.Title: 幽默是发现和享受编程的好方法，所以我建立了 ProgrammerHumor.io | Humor is a great way to discover and enjoy programming so I built ProgrammerHumor.io
Items.Description:
程序员觉得幽默的东西非常有限，就我个人而言，我有时会通过 Programming Memes 发现新技术。 我看过很多关于 Golang、Javascript 框架、Rust、Flutter 的模因，这让我更深入地研究了这些技术，有时甚至尝试了它们。

如果幽默能引导你发现新技术并激发你对新语言或框架的兴趣，那么我认为这绝对值得。

该网站包含模因、漫画和其他我们作为程序员觉得有趣的内容。 该网站会定期更新新内容。

该网站有各种子类别，您可以深入研究。

欢迎任何反馈👐🏼！

度过美好的一天🚀。



原文:

What programmers find humorous is quite limited and for me personally, I sometimes discover new technologies via Programming Memes. I've seen a lot of memes about Golang, Javascript Frameworks, Rust, Flutter which has led me to look deeper into those technologies and sometimes even try them out.

And if humor can lead you to discover new technologies and peak your interest in a new language or framework, then I think it's definitely worth it.

The site carries memes, comics, and other content that we as programmers find funny. The website is updated regularly with new content.

The site has a variety of sub-categories that you could drill down into.

Any feedback is welcome 👐🏼!

And have an amazing day 🚀.
Items.Author: prgrmmrhumor_io

Items.Title: 重新混合共享布局，第一眼 | Remix shared layouts, a first look
Items.Description:
让我们来看看这意味着什么。
基本本质是您可以支持某些出口（几乎像 React 子级），它们可以是您的应用程序的包装器。

大致：

  • 应用程序
  • 应用程序-> 管理员
  • 应用程序 -> 管理员 -> 帖子
  • 应用程序-> 管理员-> 帖子-> 新的

这些都将自上而下工作，因此您设计和添加到应用程序的任何内容都将在管理员中可用。
在管理员中添加的任何内容都将在帖子等中可用。

这个概念并不新鲜，但在制作可重用元素方面非常强大。

在 Remix 中添加我们的第一个路由器插座

让我们更改我们昨天创建的内容以反映这一点。
我们可以将管理帖子部分更改为通过共享模板工作。

我们想设计这样的东西：

[sidebar][outlet]

侧边栏是我们的帖子管理部分，出口是详细页面（新的帖子表单）。

我们要添加的第一件事是在我们的posts文件夹中名为admin.tsx的文件。
该文件将作为我们管理后面板的主要包装模板。

注意：这里有多种设计解决方案。 这是其中之一（您还可以创建一个全局管理文件夹，这可能对您的应用程序更好）

在这个管理文件中，我们想要返回一个如上所述的布局：

 export default function PostAdmin () { return ( < div className = ' mx-auto max-w-4xl ' > < h1 className = ' my-6 mb-2 border-b-2 text-center text-3xl ' > My super cool admin < /h1 > < div className = ' grid grid-cols-4 gap-6 ' > < nav className = ' col-span-4 md:col-span-1 ' > Sidebar < /nav > < main className = ' col-span-4 md:col-span-3 ' > Main section < /main > < /div > < /div > ); }

但是，这仍然没有多大作用。 让我们始终在侧边栏中添加我们的帖子，以获得快速导航方法。

我们可以使用我们之前创建的同一个加载器。

 import { json } from " @remix-run/node " ; import { Link , useLoaderData } from " @remix-run/react " ; import { getPosts } from " ~/models/post.server " ; import { LoaderFunction } from " @remix-run/node " ; type LoaderData = { posts : Awaited < ReturnType < typeof getPosts >> ; }; export const loader : LoaderFunction = async () => { return json ({ posts : await getPosts () }); }; export default function PostAdmin () { const { posts } = useLoaderData () as LoaderData ; return ( < div className = " mx-auto max-w-4xl " > < h1 className = " my-6 mb-2 border-b-2 text-center text-3xl " > My super cool admin < /h1 > < div className = " grid grid-cols-4 gap-6 " > < nav className = " col-span-4 md:col-span-1 " > < ul > { posts . map (( post ) => ( < li key = { post . slug } > < Link to = { `/posts/ ${ post . slug } ` } className = " text-blue-600 underline " > { post . title } < /Link > < /li > ))} < /ul > < /nav > <!-- Other code --> < /div > < /div > ); }

我们可以启动我们的应用程序并访问/posts/admin链接。

已经很酷了。

现在让我们添加路由器插座，以便我们可以在此布局之上提供详细的屏幕。

 import { Outlet , [... others ] } from " @remix-run/react " ; export default function PostAdmin () { return ( < div className = " mx-auto max-w-4xl " > < div className = " grid grid-cols-4 gap-6 " > <!-- other stuff --> < main className = " col-span-4 md:col-span-3 " > < Outlet /> < /main > < /div > < /div > ); }

我们在这条路线之后定义的任何东西都将在<Outlet>部分中。

例如，让我们在posts文件夹中创建一个名为admin的新文件夹。
在那里，创建一个index.tsx文件，我们将为此呈现基本的起始页面。

 import { Link } from ' @remix-run/react ' ; export default function AdminIndex () { return ( < p > < Link to = ' new ' className = ' text-blue-600 underline ' > Create a New Post < /Link > < /p > ); }

索引非常简单，因为我们只显示new表单的链接。

这将呈现以下输出。

这个插座如何覆盖甚至是默认页面非常酷。

现在让我们将new.tsx文件移动到这个admin文件夹中，看看有什么变化。

更好的是，如果我们添加新帖子，我们可以立即在侧边栏中看到它！

你可以在GitHub 上找到完整的代码。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

One super cool aspect of Remix is that it can support multiple layouts and even sub layouts.

Let's take a look at what that means.
The basic essence is that you can support certain outlets (almost like React children), which can be the wrappers of your app.

Roughly:

  • app
  • app -> admin
  • app -> admin -> posts
  • app -> admin -> posts -> new

And these will all work top-level-down, so anything you design and add to your app will be available in admin.
Anything added in admin will be available in posts and so on.

This concept is not new but super strong in making reusable elements.

Adding our first router outlets in Remix

Let us change what we created yesterday to reflect this.
We can change the admin post section to work via a shared template.

We want to design something like this:

[sidebar][outlet]

Where the sidebar is our post admin part, and the outlet is the detailed page (the new post form).

The first thing we want to add is a file called admin.tsx in our posts folder.
This file will act as the main wrapping template for our admin post-panel.

Note: There are multiple design solutions here. This is one of them (You could also create a global admin folder, which might be better for your app)

In this admin file we want to return a layout as we described above:

export default function PostAdmin() { return ( <div className='mx-auto max-w-4xl'> <h1 className='my-6 mb-2 border-b-2 text-center text-3xl'> My super cool admin </h1>
 <div className='grid grid-cols-4 gap-6'> <nav className='col-span-4 md:col-span-1'>Sidebar</nav>
 <main className='col-span-4 md:col-span-3'>Main section</main>
 </div>
 </div>
 );
}

However, this still doesn't do much. Let's always add our posts in the sidebar to have a quick navigation method.

We can use the same loader we previously created.

import { json } from "@remix-run/node";
import { Link, useLoaderData } from "@remix-run/react";
import { getPosts } from "~/models/post.server";
import { LoaderFunction } from "@remix-run/node"; type LoaderData = { posts: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getPosts>>;
}; export const loader: LoaderFunction = async () => { return json({ posts: await getPosts() });
}; export default function PostAdmin() { const { posts } = useLoaderData() as LoaderData; return ( <div className="mx-auto max-w-4xl"> <h1 className="my-6 mb-2 border-b-2 text-center text-3xl"> My super cool admin </h1>
 <div className="grid grid-cols-4 gap-6"> <nav className="col-span-4 md:col-span-1"> <ul> {posts.map((post) => ( <li key={post.slug}> <Link to={`/posts/${post.slug}`} className="text-blue-600 underline" > {post.title} </Link>
 </li>
 ))} </ul>
 </nav>
 <!-- Other code --> </div>
 </div>
 );
}

We can spool up our app and visit the /posts/admin link.

Already pretty cool.

Now let's add the router outlet so we can provide the detailed screens on top of this layout.

import {Outlet, [...others] } from "@remix-run/react"; export default function PostAdmin() { return ( <div className="mx-auto max-w-4xl"> <div className="grid grid-cols-4 gap-6"> <!-- other stuff --> <main className="col-span-4 md:col-span-3"> <Outlet /> </main>
 </div>
 </div>
 );
}

Anything we define after this route will be in the <Outlet> part.

For instance, let's create a new folder called admin inside our posts folder.
In there, create an index.tsx file, for which we'll render the basic starting page.

import { Link } from '@remix-run/react'; export default function AdminIndex() { return ( <p> <Link to='new' className='text-blue-600 underline'> Create a New Post </Link>
 </p>
 );
}

The index is pretty simple as we only show a link to the new form.

This will render the following output.

Pretty cool how this outlet can overwrite even the default page.

Now let's move our new.tsx file into this admin folder and see what changes.

And even better, if we add a new post, we get to see it in the sidebar right away!

You can find the complete code on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter
Items.Author: dailydevtips1

Items.Title: 推特机器人能否在 100 天内获得 1,000 名关注者？ 对我自己和这个社区的挑战 | Can a twitter bot get 1,000 followers in 100 days? A challenge to myself and this community
Items.Description:
昨天，我做了一个推特机器人，如果你提到它@poet_this ，它会截取推文。 令人惊讶的是，它开始获得追随者——嗯，只有 10 个追随者，但仍然如此！

所以这是挑战：
推特机器人能否在 100 天内获得 1000 个关注者？ 每天只有 10 个追随者，我相信这是可能的。

让我们看看：

  • 人们有兴趣关注一个只显示其他有趣、有趣或仅可截图的 Twitter 帐户
  • 这个社区有多强大

这是推特账号：
今天是第 2 天，第 1 天非常强劲，有 13 个追随者。

分享这个，用#PoetThisBot 发推文，尽你所能提供帮助！



原文:

Yesterday, I made a twitter bot that takes screenshots of tweets if you mention it @poet_this. Surprisingly, it started gaining followers - well, just 10 followers, but still!

So here's the challenge:
Can a twitter bot get 1000 followers in 100 days? It's just 10 followers a day, and I believe it's possible.

Let's see if:

  • People are interested in following a twitter account that shows nothing but tweets other found interesting, funny or just screenshot-able
  • How strong this community is

Here's the twitter account:
https://twitter.com/poet_this

Today is day 2, and day 1 was pretty strong with 13 followers.

Share this, tweet with #PoetThisBot, do whatever you can to help!
Items.Author: dhravya

Items.Title: 使用 AWS Lambda 函数 URL 为 Slack 构建无服务器后端 | Using AWS Lambda Function URL to build a Serverless backend for Slack
Items.Description:
它们使开发人员能够专注于他们的应用程序，而不是花时间配置和管理服务器。

但是，如果您的要求更简单，并且您只需要 Lambda 函数的 HTTP(S) 端点（例如，用作 Webhook），您可以使用Lambda 函数 URL ！ 当您创建函数 URL 时，Lambda 会自动生成一个专用于您的 Lambda 函数的唯一 HTTP(S) 终端节点。

这篇博文通过一个实际示例演示了如何使用 Lambda 函数 URL。 您将构建一个Go Lambda 函数作为Slack的无服务器 webhook 后端。

这是一个分步指南，包括：

  • 应用概述
  • 配置和部署该功能（以及一些您需要注意的问题！）
  • 如何配置 Slack 以启用端到端集成
  • 测试应用程序并玩得开心！

在本博客结束时，您将使用 Lambda 函数 URL 配置、集成和部署一个有用（并且希望很有趣？）的应用程序。 在此过程中，您将大致了解此功能，您可以在构建自己的解决方案时使用该功能！

代码在 GitHub 上可用

这个怎么运作

本博客中展示的示例应用程序是Giphy for Slack的精简版。 为了简单起见，我做了一些调整，使得无服务器后端使用Giphy Random API简单地为搜索关键字返回一个（单个）随机图像。

由于该解决方案将作为斜线命令集成到 Slack 中，因此最终用户（您！）将使用/awsome <your search term> （其中awsome只是斜线命令的名称）从 Slack 工作区调用它。 这反过来会调用 Lambda 函数 URL（该配置将在博客后面介绍），它负责其余的工作。

例如，使用/awsome serverless从您的 Slack 工作区调用它会返回一个随机 GIF（稍后您将尝试这个！）

以下是 Lambda 函数功能的概述：

  • 该函数只应该由 Slack 调用，我们需要确保我们确认这一点。 如果签名匹配成功（如果失败，我们会向客户端返回错误），解析 Slack 请求以提取用户发送的搜索text 。
  • 然后，使用搜索词调用Giphy Random API 。 如果我们得到一个成功的响应，我们会对其进行解析并以所需的格式将其发送回 Slack

最后，用户可以在他们的 Slack 工作区中看到一个 GIF！

我将跳过代码演练，以便专注于解决方案的其他方面，但函数签名值得一提——它类似于您在基于 API 网关的解决方案中使用的签名：

 func Funcy ( r events . LambdaFunctionURLRequest ) ( events . LambdaFunctionURLResponse , error ) { ... }

我们使用events.LambdaFunctionURLRequest作为输入并返回events.LambdaFunctionURLResponse 。 在幕后，Lambda 将请求映射到事件对象，然后再将其传递给函数。 最后，函数响应然后映射到 Lambda 通过函数 URL 发送回客户端的 HTTP 响应。

您可以阅读文档中的详细信息

这很方便吧？ 您可以使用 API Gateway 约定，而无需实际设置和配置一个！

有了这些背景信息，让我们继续部署功能并使用 Slack 进行尝试的部分。 但是，在此之前，请确保您已准备好以下内容：

先决条件

请记下您的 GIPHY API 密钥，因为稍后您将使用它

克隆 Github 存储库并移动到正确的目录：

 git clone https://github.com/abhirockzz/awsome-slack-backend cd awsome-slack-backend

后续步骤使用 AWS CLI - 我特意使用 AWS CLI 来突出流程的特定方面。 请查看本教程以了解 CloudFormation 和 SAM

构建、压缩和部署函数！

 export FUNC_NAME = awsome-slack-backend export FUNC_GO_BINARY_NAME = awsome export ZIP_NAME = function .zip GOOS = linux go build -o $FUNC_GO_BINARY_NAME main.go zip function .zip $FUNC_GO_BINARY_NAME

首先，为 Lambda 创建一个 IAM 角色并附加AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole策略：

 export ROLE_NAME = demo-lambda-role ROLE_ARN = $( aws iam create-role \ --role-name $ROLE_NAME \ --assume-role-policy-document '{"Version": "2012-10-17","Statement": [{ "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": {"Service": "lambda.amazonaws.com"}, "Action": "sts:AssumeRole"}]}' \ --query 'Role.[Arn]' --output text ) aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name $ROLE_NAME --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole

创建函数：

 aws lambda create-function \ --function-name $FUNC_NAME \ --runtime go1.x \ --zip-file fileb:// $ZIP_NAME \ --handler $FUNC_GO_BINARY_NAME \ --role $ROLE_ARN

创建函数后，继续添加函数 URL：

 aws lambda create-function-url-config \ --function-name $FUNC_NAME \ --auth-type NONE

出于此示例应用程序的目的，我们使用NONE作为身份验证类型。 这意味着 Lambda 函数 URL 将可公开访问 - 稍后将详细介绍

如果您导航到 AWS 控制台并打开刚刚创建的函数，您应该会看到与其关联的函数 URL：

让我们调用函数 - 复制函数 URL 并将其粘贴到浏览器中或使用任何其他工具（例如curl ）

你应该得到一个带有HTTP 403 Forbidden状态码的{"Message":"Forbidden"}响应

不用担心，这是意料之中的 - 我想确保您遇到此问题并了解根本原因。

即使我们使用NONE作为身份验证方案，用户仍然必须具有lambda:InvokeFunctionUrl权限才能成功调用函数 URL。 如果您直接使用 AWS CLI（如本博客中所述）、AWS CloudFormation 或 Lambda API，则情况并非如此 - 您必须自己添加权限。

让我们这样做：

 aws lambda add-permission \ --function-name $FUNC_NAME \ --action lambda:InvokeFunctionUrl \ --statement-id FunctionURLAllowPublicAccess \ --principal "*" \ --function-url-auth-type NONE

要查看策略，请在 AWS 控制台中导航到您的函数：配置 > 权限

再次调用该函数：

这一次，您将收到一个带有HTTP 401 Unauthorized状态代码的不同错误。 这也是意料之中的！

让我们完成其余的配置以使事情正常进行。

配置松弛

请注意，本节中的大部分说明均改编自Slack 文档

首先登录您的Slack 工作区并创建一个新的 Slack 应用程序。

完成后，创建一个斜线命令 - 前往您应用的设置页面，然后单击导航菜单中的斜线命令功能。 您将看到一个标记为Create New Command的按钮，当您单击它时，您会看到一个屏幕，要求您使用所需信息定义新的Slash Command 。

输入所需信息 - 为命令输入/awsome并在请求 URL 中输入 Lambda 函数 URL

最后，将应用程序安装到您的工作区 - 单击导航菜单中的基本信息功能，选择将应用程序安装到您的工作区，然后单击将应用程序安装到工作区。 这会将应用程序安装到您的 Slack 工作区以测试您的应用程序并生成与 Slack API 交互所需的令牌。

完成安装应用程序后，应用程序凭据将显示在同一页面上。 你需要从那里获取你的Slack Signing Secret

记下您的应用签名密钥，因为您稍后将使用它

更新功能

现在您已经有了 Slack 签名密钥，您还需要确保在函数中对其进行配置。 此外，不要忘记 GIPHY API 密钥，因为该函数需要它来调用 GIPHY REST 端点。

让我们更新函数以将它们包含为环境变量：

 aws lambda update-function-configuration \ --function-name $FUNC_NAME \ --environment "Variables={SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET=<enter Slack signing secret>,GIPHY_API_KEY=<enter Giphy API key>}"

示例应用程序使用 Lambda 环境变量来存储 Slack 和 GIPHY 的密钥 - 这仅用于演示目的。 您应该使用AWS Secrets Manager等解决方案来安全地存储和管理凭证。

你都准备好了！

前往您的 Slack 工作区并调用该命令。 例如，要获得一个随机的猫 GIF，只需输入：

我得到了这个回应。 你呢？ 随意玩这个应用程序！

清理

完成后，删除该函数以及 IAM 策略和角色。

 aws lambda delete-function --function-name $FUNC_NAME aws iam detach-role-policy --role-name $ROLE_NAME --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole aws iam delete-role --role-name $ROLE_NAME

结论

您为 Slack 配置和部署了一个无服务器后端，并在此过程中通过此示例应用程序了解了 Lambda 函数 URL 的某些方面。 我鼓励您探索其他功能，例如AWS_IAM 身份验证、 CORS 配置、节流限制、监控等。

快乐编码！



原文:

A combination of AWS Lambda and Amazon API Gateway is a widely-used architecture for serverless microservices and API based solutions. They enable developers to focus on their applications, instead of spending time provisioning and managing servers.

API Gateway is a feature rich offering that includes with support for different API types (HTTP, REST, WebSocket), multiple authentication schemes, API versioning, canary deployments and much more! However, if your requirements are simpler and all you need is an HTTP(S) endpoint for your Lambda function (for example, to serve as a webhook), you can use Lambda Function URLs! When you create a function URL, Lambda automatically generates a unique HTTP(S) endpoint that is dedicated for your Lambda function.

This blog post demonstrates how to use Lambda function URL with a practical example. You will build a Go Lambda function to serve as a serverless webhook backend for Slack.

It's a step-by-step guide that covers:

  • Overview of the application
  • Configure and deploy the function (along with some gotchas you need to watch out for!)
  • How to configure Slack to enable the end to end integration
  • Test the app and have fun!

By the end of this blog, you would have configured, integrated and deployed a useful (and hopefully fun?) app using Lambda function URL. In the process, you will get an overview of this feature that you can utilise when building your own solutions!

The code is available on GitHub

How it works

The sample app presented in this blog is a trimmed down version of Giphy for Slack. The (original) Giphy Slack app returns a bunch of GIFs for a search term and the user can pick one of them. To keep things simple, I've tweaked things a bit such that the serverless backend simply returns a (single) random image for a search keyword using the Giphy Random API.

Since the solution will be integrated as a Slash Command in Slack, the end user (you!) will invoke it from a Slack workspace using /awsome <your search term> (where awsome is nothing but the name of the slash command). This in turn invokes the Lambda function URL (the configuration is covered later in the blog), which takes care of the rest.

For example, invoking it from your Slack workspace using /awsome serverless will return a random GIF (you will try this later!)

Here is an overview of what the Lambda function does:

  • Slack slash command invocation results in a base64 encoded string payload being sent to the Lambda function URL - so the first step is to decode it.
  • The function is only supposed to invoked by Slack and we need to make sure we confirm that. Slack makes this possible by allowing apps to verify requests using a signing secret - the function simply implements a Go version of the signature matching recipe presented here
  • If the signature match is successful (we return an error to the client if it fails), the Slack request is parsed to extract the search text that user sent.
  • Then, the Giphy Random API is invoked with the search term. If we get a successful response, we parse it and send it back to Slack in it's desired format

Finally, the user gets to see a GIF in their Slack workspace!

I will skip the code walk-through in order to focus on other aspects of the solution, but the function signature deserves a mention - it is similar to what you would've used in case of an API Gateway based solution:

func Funcy(r events.LambdaFunctionURLRequest) (events.LambdaFunctionURLResponse, error) { ...
}

We are using events.LambdaFunctionURLRequest as input and returning events.LambdaFunctionURLResponse. Behind the scenes, Lambda maps the request to an event object before passing it to the function. Finally, the function response is then mapped to an HTTP response that Lambda sends back to the client through the function URL.

You can read up on the details in the documentation

That's quite convenient right? You can use API Gateway conventions without actually having to setup and configure one!

With that background info, let's move on to the part where you deploy the function and try it out with Slack. But, before that make sure you have the following ready:

Pre-requisites

Please note down your GIPHY API key as you will be using it later

Clone the Github repo and move into the right directory:

git clone https://github.com/abhirockzz/awsome-slack-backend
cd awsome-slack-backend

The subsequent steps use AWS CLI - I've purposely used the AWS CLI in order to highlight specific aspects of the process. Please check this tutorial for CloudFormation and SAM

Build, zip and deploy the function! 

export FUNC_NAME=awsome-slack-backend
export FUNC_GO_BINARY_NAME=awsome
export ZIP_NAME=function.zip GOOS=linux go build -o $FUNC_GO_BINARY_NAME main.go zip function.zip $FUNC_GO_BINARY_NAME

First, create an IAM Role for Lambda and attach the AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole policy:

export ROLE_NAME=demo-lambda-role ROLE_ARN=$(aws iam create-role \ --role-name $ROLE_NAME \ --assume-role-policy-document '{"Version": "2012-10-17","Statement": [{ "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": {"Service": "lambda.amazonaws.com"}, "Action": "sts:AssumeRole"}]}' \ --query 'Role.[Arn]' --output text) aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name $ROLE_NAME --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole

Create the function:

aws lambda create-function \ --function-name $FUNC_NAME \ --runtime go1.x \ --zip-file fileb://$ZIP_NAME \ --handler $FUNC_GO_BINARY_NAME \ --role $ROLE_ARN

After the function gets created, go ahead and add the Function URL:

aws lambda create-function-url-config \ --function-name $FUNC_NAME \ --auth-type NONE

For the purposes of this sample app, we're using NONE as the authentication type. This means that the Lambda function URL will be publicly accessible - more on this shortly

If you navigate to the AWS console and open the function you just created, you should see the Function URL associated with it:

Let's invoke the function - copy the Function URL and paste it in a browser or use any other tool (e.g. curl)

You should get a {"Message":"Forbidden"} response with a HTTP 403 Forbidden status code

Don't worry, this is expected - I wanted to make sure you encounter this issue and understand the root cause.

Even though we use NONE as the authentication scheme, users must still have lambda:InvokeFunctionUrl permissions in order to successfully invoke the function URL. The (slightly) tricky bit is that when you create a function URL (with auth type NONE) via the console or AWS Serverless Application Model (AWS SAM), Lambda automatically creates the resource-based policy statement for you (details in the documentation). That's not the case if you're using the AWS CLI (as in this blog), AWS CloudFormation, or the Lambda API directly - you must add permissions yourself.

Let's do that:

aws lambda add-permission \ --function-name $FUNC_NAME \ --action lambda:InvokeFunctionUrl \ --statement-id FunctionURLAllowPublicAccess \ --principal "*" \ --function-url-auth-type NONE

To see the policy, navigate to your Function in the AWS console: Configuration > Permissions

Invoke the function again:

This time, you will get a different error with a HTTP 401 Unauthorized status code. This is expected as well!

Let's finish the rest of the configuration to get things working.

Configure Slack

Please note that most of the instructions in this section have been adapted from the Slack documentation

Start by signing into your Slack Workspace and creating a new Slack App.

Once that's done, create a Slash Command - head to your app's settings page, and then click the Slash Commands feature in the navigation menu. You'll be presented with a button marked Create New Command, and when you click on it, you'll see a screen where you'll be asked to define your new Slash Command with the required information.

Enter the required information - Enter /awsome for the Command and enter the Lambda Function URL in Request URL

Finally, install the app to your workspace - click the Basic Information feature in the navigation menu, choose Install your app to your workspace and click Install App to Workspace. This will install the app to your Slack workspace to test your app and generate the tokens you need to interact with the Slack API.

As soon as you finish installing the app, the App Credentials will show up on the same page. You need to grab your Slack Signing Secret from there

Make a note of your app Signing Secret as you'll be using it later

Update the function

Now that you've the Slack signing secret key, you need to make sure to configure it in the function as well. Also, don't forget the GIPHY API key since the function needs that to invoke GIPHY REST endpoint.

Let's update the function to include these as environment variables:

aws lambda update-function-configuration \ --function-name $FUNC_NAME \ --environment "Variables={SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET=<enter Slack signing secret>,GIPHY_API_KEY=<enter Giphy API key>}"

The sample app uses Lambda environment variables to store keys for Slack and GIPHY - this is just for demonstration purposes. You should use a solution such as AWS Secrets Manager to securely store and manage credentials.

You're all set!

Head over to your Slack workspace and invoke the command. For example, to get a random cat GIF, just type:

I got this response. How about you? :-)

Feel free to play around with the app!

Clean up

Once you're done, delete the function along with the IAM policy and role.

aws lambda delete-function --function-name $FUNC_NAME aws iam detach-role-policy --role-name $ROLE_NAME --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole aws iam delete-role --role-name $ROLE_NAME

Conclusion

You configured and deployed a serverless backend for Slack and in the process, learnt about some of the aspects of Lambda Function URLs through the lens of this sample app. I would encourage you to explore other capabilities such as AWS_IAM authentication, CORS config, throttling limits, monitoring etc.

Happy coding!
Items.Author: abhirockzz

Items.Title: 将远程配置集成到您的 HarmonyOS 应用程序中 | Integrating Remote Configuration into Your HarmonyOS App
Items.Description:
远程配置

背景

想象一下，您想为您的应用程序添加一个新功能，但您不确定它将如何影响用户体验和使用。 您希望能够在发布完整版本之前对其进行测试。 这就是远程配置可以帮助您的地方，它允许您灵活地更改应用程序的行为和 UI，而无需发布新的应用程序版本，确保您可以灵活地响应用户需求。 该服务为多个平台提供 SDK，包括 Android、iOS、Harmony OS 和 Web。 这篇文章将说明如何集成远程配置的 HarmonyOS SDK。

准备工作

  • 配置签名：安装DevEco Studio和 HarmonyOS SDK。 安装后，在 DevEco Studio 中创建一个 Java 项目。

如果您是第一次需要安装 DevEco studio IDE，您可以按照开发人员门户中说明的步骤进行操作。

选择能力模板

  • 配置签名：在 DevEco Studio 中，转到File > Project Structure > Project > Signing Configs 。 在“调试”选项卡页面上，选择“自动生成签名”。 然后单击确定。 登录AppGallery Connect后会自动生成签名。


签署配置配置

  • 启用远程配置并在远程配置中设置所需的参数值


远程配置设置

集成远程配置 SDK

  • 目前，创建HarmonyOS应用时会自动配置maven仓库地址。 只需将 AppGallery Connect 插件添加到项目级build.gradle文件中即可。
 classpath **'com.huawei.agconnect:agcp-harmony:1.1.0.200'**
  • 在应用级build.gradle文件中应用插件。
 apply **plugin** : **'com.huawei.agconnect'**
  • 然后添加服务依赖。
 implementation **'com.huawei.agconnect:agconnect-remoteconfig-harmony:1.1.0.200'** implementation **"com.huawei.agconnect:agconnect-tasks-harmony:1.1.0.200"**

开发功能

  • 设置默认参数值：在resources > profile下创建一个 XML 文件，用于将应用内默认参数值保存在键值对中。


远程配置值

调用applyDefault以应用应用内默认参数值。

  • 调用fetch从远程配置中获取更新的参数值。

结果

应用启动后，显示应用内默认参数值。


显示默认参数

点击获取按钮后，将显示从远程配置获取的参数值。


显示远程配置参数

结论

在本文中，我们看到了将远程配置与您的 HarmonyOS 应用程序集成是多么容易。

有关 HarmonyOS 技术的更多详细背景信息，您可以访问并阅读我们在论坛上的文章。 下次见！

和谐操作系统



原文:


Remote Configuration

Background

Imagine that you want to add a new feature to your app, but you are not certain how it will affect user experience and usage. You want to be able to test it before moving forward with a full release. This is where Remote Configuration helps you, by allowing you to flexibly change the behavior and UI for your app without releasing a new app version, ensuring you can flexibly respond to user needs. The service provides SDKs for multiple platforms, including Android, iOS, Harmony OS, and web. This post will illustrate how to integrate the HarmonyOS SDK of Remote Configuration.

Preparations

  • Configuring the Signature: Install DevEco Studio and the HarmonyOS SDK. Once installed, create a Java project in DevEco Studio.

You can follow steps explained in developer portal if it is first time you need to install DevEco studio IDE.

https://developer.harmonyos.com/en/docs/documentation/doc-guides/software\_install-0000001053582415


Choose ability template

  • Configuring the Signature: In DevEco Studio, go to File > Project Structure > Project > Signing Configs. On the Debug tab page, select Automatically generate signing. Then click OK. The signature will be automatically generated after you sign in to AppGallery Connect.


Signing config configuration

  • Enabling remote configuration and setting required parameter values in Remote Configuration


Remote config setting

Integrating the Remote Configuration SDK

  • Currently, the maven repository address is automatically configured when you create a HarmonyOS app. Just simply add the AppGallery Connect plugin to the project-level build.gradle file. 
classpath **'com.huawei.agconnect:agcp-harmony:1.1.0.200'**
  • Apply the plugin in the app-level build.gradle file. 
apply **plugin** : **'com.huawei.agconnect'**
  • Then add the service dependency. 
implementation **'com.huawei.agconnect:agconnect-remoteconfig-harmony:1.1.0.200'**  

implementation **"com.huawei.agconnect:agconnect-tasks-harmony:1.1.0.200"**

Developing Functions

  • Set the default parameter values: Create an XML file for saving in-app default parameter values in key-value pairs, under resources > profile.


config.xml file


remote config value

Call applyDefault to apply the in-app default parameter values.

  • Call fetch to fetch the updated parameter values from Remote Configuration.

Results

After the app is launched, the in-app default parameter values are displayed.


Default parameter displayed

After the fetch button is tapped, the parameter values fetched from Remote Configuration are displayed instead.


Remote config param displayed

Conclusion

In this article, we saw how easy is to integrate the Remote Configuration with your HarmonyOS apps.

For more detailed background about HarmonyOS technology, you can visit and read our articles on the forum. See you next!

HarmonyOS

Items.Title: ‍ Intermediate | ‍ 中级 | HarmonyOS Push Kit & Location Kit |Earthquake Notifier App Part 2 HarmonyOS Push Kit & Location Kit |Earthquake Notifier App Part 2 | ‍ Intermediate | HarmonyOS Push Kit & Location Kit |Earthquake Notifier App Part 2
Items.Description:
介绍

在本文中，我们将继续开发 Earthquake Notifier 应用程序。 在这一部分中，我们将使用 HMS Location Kit 检索用户位置，并使用 HMS Push Kit 向用户发送通知。

如果您还没有检查第 1 部分，您可以从这里检查：

要求

你会学到什么

如何：

  • 在 HarmonyOS 上使用 HMS 定位工具包
  • 在 HarmonyOS 上使用 HMS Map Kit
  • 使用 Retrofit 检索访问令牌

发展

项目层次结构

在第 2 部分中，我觉得有必要展示项目的层次结构，因为我添加了新类并将一些方法移动到新类中。 我将与调用地震 API 相关的代码移至新类EarhquakeApiProvider 。 还添加了通知所需的新模型类。

首先，我们必须实现Location Kit。 让我们在应用级 gradle 文件中输入以下行。

我们将创建帮助类来处理名为LocationProvider的位置功能。

我们将创建一种方法来检查设备位置设置，并在位置被禁用时通知用户。

对于定位工具包，我们需要以动态方式向用户请求定位许可。 首先要做的是向config.json文件添加权限。

现在，我们将创建用于检查位置权限是否已授予以及从用户向LocationProvider类请求权限的方法。

我们将添加用于启动位置请求和停止的方法。

如您所见， startLocationUpdate方法上面的代码使用了一个间隔值。 该值表示请求用户定位间隔。 两种方法都使用LocationCallback对象。 我们将在调用此方法时实现并传递此回调对象。

这就是LocationProvider类的全部内容。 现在我们可以用这个类收集用户位置。 正如我之前所说，我稍微改变了项目结构。 将地震相关代码移至新类。 我不会进一步解释那部分，因为这篇文章不是关于那部分的。 但是，如果您想查找它，我将分享有关参考的项目。

我们想根据位置更新更改地图上的用户标记，并根据用户位置检查地震距离。

让我们跳到MainAbilitySlice ：

我们将创建LocationProvider实例和回调对象。 成功检索位置后，我们将保存用户位置并将地图相机移动到位置。 我们使用 Circle 对象来标记用户位置并使用setCenter方法移动它。 我们还将在此处检查位置设置并作为用户的位置许可。 最后我们可以调用startLocationUpdate方法。

必须从 AGC 启用推送套件。 您可以从此处查看如何启用它。

首先，我们需要在应用级别的 gradle 文件中实现 Push kit 依赖，并创建服务能力扩展HmsMessageService 。 我们还需要更新config.json文件。

为了检索访问令牌，我们需要发送发布请求。 我们将通过改造来处理这个问题。 我们将更新我们在第 1 部分创建的ApiClient和ApiInterface类。 此外，为了发送通知，我们需要发送发布请求。 我们还将为此使用 Retrofit。 您可以在下面查看基本 URL。

要检索访问令牌，您需要使用 3 个参数发布请求。 我们稍后会解释它们。 通知请求需要 2 个参数。 访问令牌模型类：

现在我们将创建处理通知相关功能的辅助类。

现在我们需要获取推送令牌：

我们需要获取访问令牌。 为了获得访问令牌，我们将调用我们创建并传递参数的改造服务。 第一个参数grantType是常量值。 要获取clientId和clientSecret ，您可以从 App Information 部分下的 AGC 获取它们。


现在我们可以发送通知了。

由于通知正文具有复杂的结构，我们将为它创建模型类。 通知正文结构：

一切就绪。 您可以通过创建NotificationSender实例并调用sendNotification方法来发送通知。 检测到新地震时将发送通知。 为此，我们需要另一个服务在后台运行并定期检查新地震。 这是另一篇文章的主题，因为它会使这篇文章太长而无法阅读。

提示与技巧

  1. 不要忘记在AppGalleryConnect上启用 Push Kit。
  2. 将 appgallery-connect.json 文件添加到：entry>src>main>resources>rawfile 目录。
  3. 您可以从 appgallery-connect.json > client/app_id 获取推送令牌的 AppId。

结论

我们与您一起在HarmonyOS上开发了一个EarthquakeNotifier应用程序。 在这个项目中，我们添加了定位工具包并不断获取用户位置，并创建通知服务在需要时向用户发送通知。 我将在 Github 中创建另一个分支，这样代码就不会与 part1 混合。 您可以在参考文献中找到项目。

感谢您的阅读。 在我的其他文章中见……；）

参考

  • GitHub - yunussevim/EarthquakeNotifier at part2 GitHub - yunussevim/EarthquakeNotifier at part2
  • Document 文档
  • Document 文档

https://developer.harmonyos.com/en/docs/documentation/doc-guides/document-outline-0000001064589184 https://developer.harmonyos.com/en/docs/documentation/doc-guides/document-outline-0000001064589184



原文:

Introduction

In this article we will continue to develop Earthquake Notifier app. In this part we will retrieve user location with HMS Location Kit and send notifications to the user with HMS Push Kit.

If you haven’t checked part1 you can check it from here:

👨🏼‍💻Intermediate: HarmonyOS Earthquake Notifier App Part 1

Requirements

What Will You Learn

How to:

  • Use HMS Location Kit on HarmonyOS
  • Use HMS Map Kit on HarmonyOS
  • Retrieve access token with Retrofit

Development

1- Project Hierarchy


Project Hierarchy

In part2, I felt the need to show the hierarchy of projects because of I added new classes and move some methods to new classes. I moved code related to the calling earthquake API to new class EarhquakeApiProvider. Also added new model classes needed for notifications.

2- Location Kit

First of all, we must implement the Location Kit. Let’s enter the following lines into the app level gradle file.

We will create helper class for handling location functionalities named LocationProvider.

We will create a method checks device location settings and inform user if locations are disabled.

For location kit we need to ask location permission from user in a dynamic way. First thing to do is add permission to the config.json file.

Now, we will create methods for checking location permission is granted and requesting permissions from user to LocationProvider class.

Location Kit has a feature that retrieves location in dedicated intervals. We will add to method for starting location requests and stopping.

As you see code above startLocationUpdate method uses a interval value. This value indicates requesting user location interval. Also both method uses LocationCallback object. We will implement and pass this callback object while calling this methods.

That’s all for LocationProvider class. Now we can gather user location with this class. As I said before I changed project structure little bit. Move earthquake related code to new class. I wont explain further about that part since this article isn’t about that. But if you want to look for that I will share project on references.

We want to change user marker on map according to the location update and check earthquakes distance with user location.

Let’s jump to MainAbilitySlice :

We will create LocationProvider instance and callback object. When location retrieved successfully we will save user location and move map camera to location. We use Circle object to mark user location and move it with setCenter method. Also we will check location settings and as location permission from user here. Finally we can call startLocationUpdate method.

3- Push Kit

Push kit must be enabled from AGC. You can see how to enable it from here.

Firstly we need to implement Push kit dependency into the app level gradle file and create service ability extends HmsMessageService. Also we need to update config.json file.

Push Kit requires access token and push token in order to send notifications. In order to retrieve access token we need to send post request. We will handle this with retrofit. We will update ApiClient and ApiInterface classes that we create on part1. Also to sending notifications we need to send post request. We will also use Retrofit for it. You can see base URLs below.

To retrieve access token you need to post request with 3 parameters. We will explain them later. Notification request needs 2 parameters. Auth parameter is access token that we retrieve above and notificationMessage body part of request that holds notification data.

Access token model class:

Now we will create helper class that handles notification related functionalities.

Now we need to get push token:

And we need to get access token. In order to get access token we will call retrofit service we create and pass parameters. First parameter grantType is constant value. To get clientId and clientSecret you can get them from AGC under App Information section.


AGC: client ID and client secret

Now we can send notifications.

Since notification body has complex structure we will create model class for it. Notification body structure:

It’s all set. You can send notifications with creating instance of NotificationSender and calling sendNotification method. Notifications will be sent when new earthquakes detected. To do that we need another service runs background and checks new earthquakes in intervals. This is another articles’ topic since it’d make this article too long to read.

Tips & Tricks

  1. Don’t forget to enable Push Kit on AppGalleryConnect.
  2. Add the appgallery-connect.json file to the: entry>src>main>resources>rawfile directory.
  3. You can get AppId for push token from appgallery-connect.json > client/app_id.

Conclusion

We have developed a EarthquakeNotifier app on HarmonyOS with you. As in this project, We added location kit and get user location continuously and create notification service sends notifications to user when needed. I will create another branch in Github so code won’t mixed with part1. You can find project on references.

Thank you for reading. See you in my other articles…;)

References

  • GitHub - yunussevim/EarthquakeNotifier at part2
  • Document
  • Document

https://developer.harmonyos.com/en/docs/documentation/doc-guides/document-outline-0000001064589184
Items.Author: hdgurkey

Items.Title: Kubernetes 存储模式 | Kubernetes Storage Patterns
Items.Description:
应用程序开发团队现在可以在短短几天内开发测试并在各种环境中部署他们的应用程序，所有这些都在 Kubernetes 集群中。 过去，技术通常需要数月，甚至数周。

这种加速是由于 Kubernetes 提供了对等式的抽象，即它处理虚拟机或物理机的基础，允许用户指定 CPU、内存、容器数量和其他参数。 它得到了一个庞大的家庭和不断增长的使用的支持； 您是否想进一步了解 Kubernetes 的深入知识和实践经验，例如设置自己的 Kubernetes 集群、配置 pod 之间的网络以及保护集群免受未经授权的访问？ 它将通过动手演示帮助您理解关键的 Kubernetes 概念。 您应该检查Kubernetes 认证。

现代模型将计算和存储分开。 要充分理解 Kubernetes 情况下的分解概念，有必要了解有状态应用程序和存储的概念。 这就是为什么 S3 提供的 RESTful API 方法比其他解决方案提供的传统 POSIX/CSI 模型具有明显优势的原因。

本文重点介绍 Kubernetes 存储模式，并讨论无状态与有状态的争论，以准确理解区别及其含义。 以下文章讨论了多个应用程序关于容器和 Kubernetes 最佳实践的各种存储模式。

无状态容器

容器是轻量级且不可复制的。 它们可以在几秒钟内停止、删除或转移到另一个节点。 这可能每天都在一个广泛的编排框架中发生，而用户不会注意到这些变化。 但是，这只有在容器不依赖于它所基于的节点上的数据时才有可能。 绝不是无状态的容器。

有状态的容器

如果容器有关于本地安装的磁盘（或块设备）的信息，则存储需要移动到新节点和容器以防发生故障。 这是必不可少的，否则容器中运行的软件将无法正常运行，因为它必须引用本地存储设备上存储的数据。 这些容器是有状态的。
云原生应用程序设计

传统上，应用程序使用数据库来存储结构化数据，并使用本地驱动器或分布式系统来转储其所有非结构化和半结构化文件。 随着非结构化数据量的增加，开发人员意识到 POSIX 并不是一个好的选择，而且高额的间接税阻碍了应用程序达到所需的规模。

这会产生大量不会增加价值的开销。 对象存储 API（例如 S3 API）消除了这些限制，允许应用程序触发并忘记请求。 来自存储设备的消息将指示呼叫是成功还是不成功。 如果操作失败，应用程序可以重试。

网络的限制

在分布式系统的情况下，这意味着可能有多个应用程序竞争数据写入一个挂载。 因此，除了让应用程序竞争带宽（将文件传输到驱动器）之外，存储设备本身也在同一网络内竞争带宽以移动到虚拟驱动器。 由于 POSIX 聊天，对网络的呼叫量增加了很多倍。 安全：

这使得适应基于云的世界变得具有挑战性。 现代应用程序依赖于基于策略定义的访问帐户、临时凭证、服务帐户等的 API 内置安全模型。

可管理性：

有状态的容器增加了管理的负担。 同步并行数据访问，确保数据一致性等，需要仔细分析数据访问模式。 这将需要额外的应用程序来管理、安装和配置以及额外的开发工作。



原文:

Kubernetes has revolutionized the conventional app development and deployment methods. Applications development teams can now develop tests and deploy their applications in just a few days, in various environments, all in the Kubernetes clusters. In the past, technology generally required months, if not weeks.

This speed-up is due to the abstraction Kubernetes offers into the equation, i.e., it takes care of the fundamentals of virtual or physical machines, allowing users to specify CPU, memory, the number of containers, and other parameters. It is backed by a massive family of people and growing use; Kubernetes is the leading container orchestration platform by a substantial amount.

Are you looking to know more about Kubernetes' in-depth knowledge and hands-on experiences, such as setting up your own Kubernetes Cluster, configuring networking between pods, and securing the cluster against unauthorized access? It will help you to understand critical Kubernetes concepts with hands-on demonstrations. You should check for Kubernetes certification.

Kubernetes is the latest model for application development and deployment, and management. Modern models separate computation and storage. To fully comprehend the concept of disaggregation within the Kubernetes situation, it is necessary to be aware of the notions of stateful applications and storage. This is why the RESTful API approach offered by S3 has distinct advantages over the traditional POSIX/CSI model offered by other solutions.

This article focuses on the Kubernetes storage pattern and discusses the debate of stateless vs. stateful to precisely understand the distinction and what it means. The following post discusses the various storage patterns of multiple applications about containers and Kubernetes best practices.

Stateless containers

Containers are lightweight and non-replicable. They can be stopped, deleted, or transferred to another node in seconds. This can happen every day in an extensive orchestration framework without users noticing the changes. However, this is only possible when the container does not depend on data on the node it's based on. Containers that are in no way stateless.

Stateful containers

If a container has information on locally mounted disks (or block devices), the storage needs to be moved to the new node and the container in the event of a malfunction. This is essential, or the software running in the container will not function correctly since it must refer to the data is storing locally on local storage devices. These containers are stateful.
Cloud-Native Application Design

Traditionally, applications utilized databases to store structured data and the local drive or distributed system for dumping all their unstructured and semi-structured files. As the volume of unstructured data increased, developers realized POSIX was not a good choice, and a significant overhead tax prevented the application from achieving the scale it needed to.

POSIX chatty

POSIX is a semantic system that requires every operation to be accompanied by files and metadata that ensure the particular operation's status. This creates an enormous amount of overhead that does not add value. Object storage APIs, such as S3 API, remove these restrictions, allowing applications to fire and forget about the request. The message from the storage device will indicate whether the call is successful or unsuccessful. If the action fails, the application can try again.

Limitations of networks

In the case of a distributed system, it's implied that there could be several applications competing for data writing to one mount. Thus, in addition to having the applications compete for bandwidth (to transfer files to the drive), the storage device itself competes for bandwidth within the same network to move to the virtual drives. Because of POSIX chat, the volume of calls to the network increases by many times over. S3 API is a different way of allowing a clear separation of calls made by the network between the client to server and the internal calls to servers.

Security:

The POSIX security system was created specifically for humans Administrators set the appropriate security levels for each group or user. This makes it challenging to adjust to a cloud-based world. Modern applications rely on API built-in security models based on policy-defined access accounts, temporary credentials, service accounts, and more.

Manageability:

Stateful containers increase the burden of managing. Syncing parallel data access, ensuring data consistency, etc., requires careful analysis of data access patterns. This will require additional applications to manage, install and configure and the extra development effort.
Items.Author: varunsngh007

Assalomu alaykum, bugun sizlar bilan JavaScriptda getter va setter metodlari tanishamiz.

Barchamizga tanish bo'lgan ob'ektlarning ikki xil xususiyati bo'ladi.

Birinchisi, ma'lumot xususiyati (data property). Ma'lumot xususiyati bilan ko'pchiligimiz tanishmiz va barchamiz u bilan ishlaganmiz.

Ikkinchisi kiruvchi xususiyat (accessor property). Bu tashqaridan qaraganda oddiy property ga o`xshaydigan, _property_ning qiymatini olish hamda yangi qiymat berish uchun mo'ljallangan maxsus funksiya.

getter va setter larni 3 xil usulda hosil qilish mumkin:

  • get va set kalit so`zlari orqali
  • Object.defineProperty() metodi orqali

Items.Description:
对于这个世界上的每个人来说，互联网也令人兴奋。 在这个时代，如果适当地利用互联网，它往往会成为企业的独特优势。 在线可见性是一个很少有人知道的程序，因此他们的业务需要一些投资才能提高警惕。 反对是非常激烈的，一个错误的行为可能会完全停止你的职业。 目前，每种工具对于发展业务都至关重要，但主要的是互联网的帮助。 在不使用互联网的情况下，丹佛数字营销机构无法获得。

互联网是各种文化和各种个人之间正常的主要事物。 在线可见性是在线进步的另一个名称。 大型企业的每个独立企业都知道在线可感知性的卓越价值。

了解在线可感知性如何为您提供帮助？
虽然每个人都在增加业务的曝光率，但可以在某些设备的帮助下毫不费力地做到这一点。 在线可见性旨在保护和获取您的业务信息，同时鼓励发展。 它通常向您保证，您将在搜寻订单的主页上布置看跌期权。 这对您的业务来说可能是一项巨大的成就，无论何时完成，在确认之后都不会失败。

Items.Title: 用真实数据播种开发数据库的工具 | A tool to seed your dev database with real data
Items.Description:
罗马式 P。

发表于一群开发人员和我自己创建了RepliByte - 一个开源工具，用于从生产数据库中播种开发数据库。

特点⚡

  • 支持PostgreSQL、MySQL和MongoDB的数据备份和恢复
  • 用虚假数据替换敏感数据
  • 适用于大型数据库（> 10GB）（阅读设计）
  • 数据库子集：将生产数据库缩小到更合理的大小
  • 在单个命令中使用 prod 数据启动本地数据库
  • 动态数据（解）压缩（Zlib）
  • 即时数据解密/加密 (AES-256)
  • 完全无状态（无服务器，无守护程序）和轻量级二进制文件
  • 使用自定义转换器

我的动力

作为开发人员，创建用于运行测试的假数据集是乏味的。 另外，它不反映真实世界的数据，并且很难保持更新。 如果您更喜欢使用生产数据运行应用测试。 那么 RepliByte 也适合您。

适用于 MacOSX、Linux 和 Windows。

https://github.com/qovery/replibyte give a ⭐ if you like it. https://github.com/qovery/replibyte如果您喜欢，请给个 ⭐。



原文:

Romaric P.

Posted on Apr 28

A bunch of developers and myself have created RepliByte - an open-source tool to seed a development database from a production database.

Features ⚡

  • Support data backup and restore for PostgreSQL, MySQL and MongoDB
  • Replace sensitive data with fake data
  • Works on large database (> 10GB) (read Design)
  • Database Subsetting: Scale down a production database to a more reasonable size
  • Start a local database with the prod data in a single command
  • On-the-fly data (de)compression (Zlib)
  • On-the-fly data de/encryption (AES-256)
  • Fully stateless (no server, no daemon) and lightweight binary
  • Use custom transformers

My motivation

As a developer, creating a fake dataset for running tests is tedious. Plus, it does not reflect the real-world data and painful to keep updated. If you prefer to run your app tests with production data. Then RepliByte is for you as well.

Available for MacOSX, Linux and Windows.

https://github.com/qovery/replibyte give a ⭐ if you like it.

Items.Title: AWS 认证数据库专业考试终极指南 | Ultimate Guide to AWS Certified Database Specialty Exam
Items.Description:
保持你的帽子，因为有一个新的 AWS 认证。

数据已超过黄金成为地球上最有价值的商品。 难怪随着越来越多的企业尝试利用他们的专业知识，对具有数据库管理专业知识的云工作者的需求也在增加。

亚马逊推出了AWS Certified Database Specialty认证，这是第一个专注于数据库技术的 AWS 认证，以确保其认证产品能够恰当地代表云生态系统的实际需求。

据 AWS 称，新的认证将证明“完全了解 AWS 数据库服务的广度以及如何加速使用数据库技术来实现业务转型”。 它旨在评估从事数据库相关专业的人员的能力。

参与者可以预料会遇到有关 AWS 数据库服务设计、迁移、部署和维护的问题，以及有关访问、安全和优化等关键要素的问题，以及如何自动化与数据库相关的管理活动。

该认证适用于使用本地和 AWS 数据库的任何人。

此认证适用于谁？

它们不适合胆小的人。 您需要扎实的数据库技术应用知识才能获得此徽章。

该考试没有先决条件，但 AWS 建议您具备：

  • 需要五年熟悉标准数据库技术。
  • 需要至少两年的 AWS 实践经验。
  • 使用本地和基于 AWS 云的关系和 NoSQL 数据库是一个优势。

数据库管理员、数据平台工程师、数据库开发人员和解决方案架构师是该资格的最常见候选人。

您需要什么信息？

此认证考试分为五个主题领域，每个领域的权重都反映了涵盖该主题的考试百分比。

领域 1 涵盖特定于工作负载的数据库设计，并占考试的大部分内容。

该领域将测试您为各种类型的数据和工作负载选择最佳数据库工具或服务的能力，以及您如何确定最佳灾难恢复和高可用性技术。

您还需要展示您构建高性能、合规和可扩展的数据库解决方案的能力，以及您为数据库解决方案定价的能力，以确保您为公司获得最大价值。

领域 2 是关于部署和移动的，其中包括自动化数据库部署、导入数据准备以及执行和验证成功的数据迁移。

领域 3 包括与管理和运营相关的所有内容。 这是维护数据库并确保正确优化数据库的日常操作。

您必须展示您执行数据库维护任务和流程、设计备份和恢复计划以及管理数据库操作环境的能力。

监控和故障排除属于第四个领域。 要在本节中取得好成绩，您需要知道如何创建和实施能够准确跟踪数据库运行状况的监控和警报系统。

您还需要能够对一些最常见的数据库问题进行故障排除和处理，并优化数据库以获得最佳性能。

最后但同样重要的是，第 5 域涵盖了数据库安全。本节将解决诸如加密传输中和静态数据、设置访问和身份验证策略以及评估适当的审计解决方案等问题。

您还必须证明您可以发现潜在的安全漏洞并知道如何修复它们。

即使是经验最丰富的云数据库专业人士也不太可能熟悉所有这些数据库，因此阅读 AWS 主要数据库产品的常见问题解答是一个不错的起点：

关于考试

参加此专业级别考试的费用为 300 美元。

这是一项多项选择考试，您必须在官方考试中心参加。 您将有 180 分钟的时间完成考试，并且需要 75% 的及格分数。

你会知道你是否马上就去世了； 如果第一次没有通过，14天后可以重新参加考试，而且没有次数限制。

重考没有节省。 因此，每次尝试都将花费您全部的注册费。

我应该怎么做才能做好准备？

例如，四小时的 AWS数据库产品课程可以在线完成，涵盖各种数据库技术和架构，然后再让您接触 AWS 数据库服务。

尽管 AWS 建议您已经完成 AWS Cloud Practitioner Essentials 课程，但本课程没有正式的先决条件。

如果您正在考虑获得 AWS Certified Database – Specialty 认证，请考虑以下一些资源：

现在提供免费的考试准备课程，可帮助您准备 AWS Certified Database - Specialty 考试。 该课程包括考试的概述结构、领域领域和示例问题。

如果您更喜欢向讲师学习，可以注册 AWS 课堂课程。

这些可以在美国、欧洲、俄罗斯、日本和墨西哥的各个地方亲自参加，也可以通过虚拟教室在线参加。

这些为期三天的培训费用约为 2,100 美元。

您将在 AWS 上的规划和设计数据库课程中学习如何规划和设计关系和非关系数据库，以及在 Amazon EC2 上托管数据库的设计注意事项。

它包括 Amazon RDS、Amazon Aurora 和 Amazon Redshift 关系数据库服务、Amazon DocumentDB、Amazon DynamoDB、Amazon ElastiCache、Amazon Neptune 和 Amazon QLDB 非关系数据库服务。

练习考试

在完成上述所有材料并为考试做好充分准备后，请进行练习考试的磨练。 您可以从Study4Exam获得真实的练习考试。 他们的 AWS Certified Database Specialty 实践考试包含基于场景的真实问题。 他们有许多可定制的选项，可帮助您以多种方式练习考试。 并帮助您有效地管理考试时间。 强烈推荐他们！

现在是你的时间

考试注册向公众开放，那么为什么不在您的收藏中添加另一个呢？ 我希望这篇文章能帮助你准备和评估你的知识。 祝你考试顺利！



原文:

Keep your hats on because there's a new AWS certification on the block.

Data has surpassed gold as the most valuable commodity on the planet. It's no wonder that demand for cloud workers with database administration expertise is increasing as more businesses attempt to exploit theirs.

Amazon has launched the AWS Certified Database Specialty certification, the first AWS credential to focus solely on database technology to ensure that its certification offerings appropriately represent the practical needs of the cloud ecosystem.

According to AWS, the new certification will certify "a complete understanding of the breadth of AWS database services and how to accelerate the use of database technology to achieve business transformation," according to AWS. It is designed to assess the abilities of those working in database-related professions.

Participants can anticipate encountering questions about AWS database services design, migration, deployment, and maintenance, and questions about key elements including access, security, and optimization, and how to automate database-related admin activities.

The certification is valid for anyone who works with on-premises and AWS databases.

For Whom is this Certification?

AWS Specialty certifications were created to certify in-depth expert knowledge in various specialized cloud disciplines. They're not for the faint of heart; you'll need a solid working knowledge of database technology to earn this badge.

The exam has no prerequisites, but AWS does recommend that you have:

  • Five years of familiarity with standard database technology is required.
  • AWS hands-on experience of at least two years is required.
  • Working with on-premises and AWS Cloud-based relational and NoSQL databases is a plus.

Database Administrators, Data Platform Engineers, Database Developers, and Solutions Architects are the most common candidates for this qualification.

What information do you require?

The exam for this certification is divided into five subject areas, each weighted to reflect the percentage of the test that will cover that topic.

Domain 1 covers Workload-Specific Database Design and accounts for most of the exam.

This domain will test your ability to choose the optimal database tool or service for various types of data and workloads and how you identify the best disaster recovery and high availability techniques.

You'll also need to demonstrate your ability to build high-performing, compliant, and scalable database solutions and your ability to cost database solutions to guarantee you're receiving the most value for your company.

Domain 2 is all about Deployment and Movement, which includes automating database deployment, data preparation for import, and performing and validating a successful data migration.

Domain 3 includes everything related to management and operations. This is the day-to-day operation of maintaining a database and ensuring that it is correctly optimized.

You'll have to demonstrate your ability to perform database maintenance tasks and processes, design backup and restore plans and manage the database's operational environment.

Monitoring and troubleshooting fall under the fourth domain. To ace this section, you'll need to know how to create and implement monitoring and alerting systems that accurately track your database's health.

You'll also need to be able to troubleshoot and handle some of the most frequent database issues and optimize the database for maximum performance.

Last but not least, Database Security is covered by Domain 5. This section will address issues such as encrypting data in transit and at rest, setting access and authentication policies, and evaluating the appropriate audit solutions.

You'll also have to demonstrate that you can spot potential security flaws and know how to repair them.

AWS has a large range of database products and services, and any of them could show up on this exam.

Even the most seasoned cloud database pro is unlikely to be familiar with all of these databases, so going through the FAQs for AWS's major database offerings is a good place to start:

Concerning the Exam

DBS-C01 is the exam you must pass to acquire this certification. It costs $300 to take this Specialty-level exam.

It's a multiple-choice exam that you'll have to take at an official testing center. You'll have 180 minutes to complete the exam, and a passing score of 75% is required.

You'll know whether you passed straight away; if you don't pass the first time, you can retake the test after 14 days, and there are no limits to how many times you can take it.

There are no savings on re-sits. Therefore, each attempt will cost you the entire registration fee.

What should I do to get ready?

Many of the training alternatives provided by AWS to help you prepare for the AWS Certified Database – Specialty test are free and may be completed on your own time.

For example, the four-hour AWS Database Offerings course may be completed online and covers a variety of database technologies and architectures before exposing you to AWS database services.

Although AWS recommends that you have already completed the AWS Cloud Practitioner Essentials course, there are no formal prerequisites for this course.

The AWS Learning Library has several short digital courses that might help you brush up on your database skills.

If you're considering pursuing the AWS Certified Database – Specialty certification, here are a few resources to consider:

A free exam preparation course is now available to help you prepare for the AWS Certified Database - Specialty exam. The course includes the exam's overview structure, domain areas, and example questions.

You can enroll in an AWS classroom course if you prefer to learn from an instructor.

These can be taken in person at various places throughout the United States, Europe, Russia, Japan, and Mexico, or online through a virtual classroom.

The cost of these three-day training is roughly $2,100.

You'll learn how to plan and design relational and nonrelational databases in the Planning and Designing Databases on AWS course and design considerations for hosting databases on Amazon EC2.

It encompasses Amazon RDS, Amazon Aurora, and Amazon Redshift relational database services, Amazon DocumentDB, Amazon DynamoDB, Amazon ElastiCache, Amazon Neptune, and Amazon QLDB nonrelational database services.

Practice Exams

After going through all the material mentioned above and completely preparing for your exam, go through the grinding of practice exams. You can get authentic practice exams from Study4Exam. Their AWS Certified Database Specialty practice exams contain scenario-based real questions. They have many customizable options that help you practice your exam in many ways. And help you manage your time in exams efficiently. Highly recommend them!

Now It's Your Time

Exam registration is open to the general public, so why not add another to your collection? I hope that this post was useful in helping you prepare and assess your knowledge. Best of luck with your exam!
Items.Author: robertjames1236789

Items.Title: 如何使用 MySQL 构建 Node.js REST API | How to Build Node.js REST API with MySQL
Items.Description:
在本教程中，您将构建一个用于添加、更新和删除待办事项的简单数据库，本教程将向您展示如何使用 Express 和 MySQL 创建 REST API。 可以克隆此项目的 GitHub 存储库以进行跟进。 让我们开始吧，好吗？

入门

本教程是一个动手演示。 在开始之前，请确保您已准备好以下内容：

  • 已安装 MySQL 数据库
  • 安装了 Node.js

什么是 MySQL 数据库

它是最常使用 PHP 的数据库系统。 它使用普通的 SQL。

  • 项目设置

    现在我们已经探索了 MySQL 数据库的全部内容，让我们开始创建我们的 RESTFul 应用程序。 首先，我们需要使用以下命令为我们的项目创建一个文件夹：

     mkdir rest-todos && cd rest-todos

    上面的代码将创建一个 rest-todos 文件夹并将当前目录更改为它。 然后，使用下面的命令，我们将创建一个新的 node.js 项目：

    接下来，我们将安装我们项目所需的依赖项。

     npm install express MySQL cors

    创建我们的 Express 服务器

    现在我们已经安装了依赖项，让我们创建一个app.js文件并在下面添加以下代码片段。 我们将导入以下内容：

     const express = require("express"); const cors = require("cors"); const AppError = require("./appError"); const errorHandler = require("./errorHandler");

    接下来，我们从 express 创建一个应用实例，使用我们应用中的 express.json() 中间件来解析 URL 编码的正文。 最后，我们让 API 路由器中间件监听到指定 URL 的传入请求。

    然后，我们检查端点中缺少的 URL，如果它们被访问，则向用户抛出 404 错误。 全局错误处理程序将处理我们将在后面的部分中设置它。

     app.use(api, router); app.all("*", (req, res, next) => { next(new AppError(`The URL ${req.originalUrl} does not exists`, 404)); }); app.use(errorHandler); const PORT = 3000; app.listen(PORT, () => { console.log(`server running on port ${PORT}`); }); module.exports = app;

    连接到 MySQL

    现在让我们继续设置我们的 MySQL 数据库。 首先，我们将使用以下命令打开我们的 MySQL shell：

    上面的命令将提示您输入 root 密码。 输入密码并按 Enter 键继续。

    然后，通过运行下面的 SQL 语句从您的 MySQL shell 创建一个数据库。

    创建数据库

    然后，通过运行以下 SQL 语句创建任务列表表。 该表将具有 id、name、status、date_created 字段。 接下来，创建一个dbConfig.js文件并将以下代码片段添加到其中以将应用程序连接到 MySQL 数据库。

     const mysql = require('mysql'); const connection = mysql.createConnection({ host: "localhost", user: "root", password: "1234", database: "todo", }); connection.connect(); module.exports = connection;

    创建我们的控制器

    让我们开始构建我们的应用程序的路由。

    在我们的项目根目录中创建一个控制器文件夹，然后在控制器文件夹中创建一个app.js文件。

    我们的全局错误处理程序和 MySQL 数据库连接将首先被导入。

     const AppError = require("./appError"); const conn = require("./dbConfig");

    下一步是设置我们的getAll处理程序，它将检索我们数据库中的所有待办事项。 此处理程序中将使用 MySQL 查询方法，该处理程序将 SQL 查询和回调函数作为输入。 如果操作期间发生错误，我们将使用AppError类向用户返回错误。

     exports.getAll = (req, res, next) => { conn.query("SELECT * FROM tasklist", function (err, data, fields) { if(err) return next(new AppError(err)) res.status(200).json({ status: "success", length: data?.length, data: data, }); }); };

    接下来，创建我们的createTask处理程序以将新的待办事项添加到我们的数据库中。 首先，我们需要在保存数据之前检查用户是否发送了一个空表单。

     exports.createTask = (req, res, next) => { if (!req.body) return next(new AppError("No form data found", 404)); const values = [req.body.name, "pending"]; conn.query( "INSERT INTO tasklist (name, status) VALUES(?)", [values], function (err, data, fields) { if (err) return next(new AppError(err, 500)); res.status(201).json({ status: "success", message: "todo created!", }); } ); };

    然后，为了通过 ID 获取待办事项，我们开发了一个 getTask 处理程序。 首先，我们将查看请求字段中是否提供了 id，如果没有，我们将向客户端返回错误。

     exports.getTask = (req, res, next) => { if (!req.params.id) { return next(new AppError("No todo id found", 404)); } conn.query( "SELECT * FROM tasklist WHERE id = ?", [req.params.id], function (err, data, fields) { if (err) return next(new AppError(err, 500)); res.status(200).json({ status: "success", length: data?.length, data: data, }); } ); };

    接下来，为了更新我们的待办事项，我们将编写我们的 updateTask 处理程序，它将编辑待完成的 id 在请求参数中的待办事项。

     exports.updateTask = (req, res, next) => { if (!req.params.id) { return next(new AppError("No todo id found", 404)); } conn.query( "UPDATE tasklist SET status='completed' WHERE id=?", [req.params.id], function (err, data, fields) { if (err) return next(new AppError(err, 500)); res.status(201).json({ status: "success", message: "todo updated!", }); } ); };

    最后，为了从我们的数据库中删除一个待办事项，我们将创建一个 deleteTask 处理程序。 要删除 id 为请求参数的待办事项，我们将使用 delete 语句。

     exports.deleteTask = (req, res, next) => { if (!req.params.id) { return next(new AppError("No todo id found", 404)); } conn.query( "DELETE FROM tasklist WHERE id=?", [req.params.id], function (err, fields) { if (err) return next(new AppError(err, 500)); res.status(201).json({ status: "success", message: "todo deleted!", }); } ); }

    创建我们的错误处理程序

    现在我们已经设置了所有 API 控制器，让我们继续使用下面的代码片段创建我们的错误类函数：

     class AppError extends Error { constructor(msg, statusCode) { super(msg); this.statusCode = statusCode; this.error = `${statusCode}`.startsWith('4') ? 'fail' : 'error'; this.isOperational = true; Error.captureStackTrace(this, this.constructor); } } module.exports = AppError;

    然后，在不停止我们的程序的情况下，检查可能的问题并将相关的错误和状态代码传递给客户端。

     module.exports = (err, req, res, next) => { err.statusCode = err.statusCode || 500; err.status = err.status || "error"; res.status(err.statusCode).json({ status: err.status, message: err.message, }); };

    创建路线

    现在让我们创建 API 路由来访问我们的控制器。 创建一个 router.js 文件并添加以下代码片段：

     const express = require("express"); const controllers = require("./controllers"); const router = express.Router(); router.route("/").get(controllers.getAllTodos).post(controllers.createTodo); router .route("/:id") .get(controllers.getTodo) .put(controllers.updateTodo) .delete(controllers.deleteTodo); module.exports = router;

    现在更新 app.js 文件以使用以下代码片段导入我们的路由器：

     ... const router = require("./router") app.use("task/", router); ...

    结论

    在本教程中，您学习了如何通过创建 todo 应用程序在 Node.js 中构建 REST API。 您向刚刚构建的应用程序添加更多功能，例如身份验证和授权。



    • 原文:

    In this tutorial, you'll build a simple database for adding, updating, and removing todos, this tutorial will show you how to create a REST API with Express and MySQL. The GitHub repository for this project can be cloned to follow along. Let's get started, shall we?

    Getting started

    This tutorial is a hands-on demonstration. Be sure you have the following in place before getting started:

    • MySQL Database installed
    • Node.js installed

    What is MySQL databases

    MySQL is an open-source relational database management system (RDBMS) (RDBMS). It's the most often used database system with PHP. MySQL is a cloud-native database solution that comes with full management. HeatWave, a built-in high-speed query accelerator, increases MySQL performance by 5400x.

    Oracle Corporation created, distributed, and maintained MySQL, which has the following functionalities.

    The data in a MySQL database is organized into tables with columns and rows.

    • MySQL is a server-based database management system.
    • MySQL is a great choice for both small and large projects.
    • MySQL is a database system that is extremely quick, dependable, and simple to use. It makes use of normal SQL.
    • MySQL runs on a variety of platforms.

    Project Setup

    Now that we have explored what MySQL database is all about, let's dive into creating our RESTFul application. First, we need to create a folder for our project with the command below:

    mkdir rest-todos && cd rest-todos

    The above code will create a rest-todos folder and change the current directory to it. Then, using the command below, we'll create a new node.js project:

    Next, we'll install the dependencies we require for our project.

    npm install express MySQL cors

    Creating our Express server

    Now that we have our dependencies installed let's create an app.js file and add the following code snippet below to it. We'll import the following:

    const express = require("express");
const cors = require("cors");
const AppError = require("./appError");
const errorHandler = require("./errorHandler");

    Next, we create an app instance from express, using the express.json() middleware in our app to parse the URL encoded body. Finally, we make our API router middleware listen to incoming requests to the URL specified.

    Then, we check for URLs missing from our endpoints and throw a 404 error to the user if they're accessed. The global error handler will handler we will have it setup in a later section.

    app.use(api, router);

app.all("*", (req, res, next) => {
 next(new AppError(`The URL ${req.originalUrl} does not exists`, 404));
});
app.use(errorHandler);

const PORT = 3000;
app.listen(PORT, () => {
 console.log(`server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

module.exports = app;

    connecting to MySQL

    Now let's go ahead and set up our MySQL Database. First, we'll open our MySQL shell with the command below:

    The above command will prompt for your root password. Enter the password and press the Enter key to continue.

    Then, create a database from your MySQL shell with by running the SQL statements below.

    CREATE DATABASE

    Then, create the tasklist table by running the SQL statements below. The table will have an id, name, status, date_created fields. The id is the primary key of our table.

    CREATE TABLE tasklist(id int NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
name varchar(50) NOT NULL, 
status varchar(50), 
date_created DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, 
PRIMARY KEY (id));

    Next, create a dbConfig.js file and add the following code snippets below to it to connect the application to MySQL database.

    const mysql = require('mysql');
const connection = mysql.createConnection({
 host: "localhost",
 user: "root",
 password: "1234",
 database: "todo",
});

connection.connect();

module.exports = connection;

    Create our controllers

    Let's get started building our application's routes.

    Create a controllers folder in our project root directory, then an app.js file in the controllers folder.

    Our global error handler and MySQL database connection will be imported first.

    const AppError = require("./appError");
const conn = require("./dbConfig");

    The next step is to setup our getAll handler, which will retrieve all of the todos in our database. The MySQL query method will be used in this handler, which takes a SQL query and a callback function as inputs. We'll use the AppError class to return an error to the user if an error happens during the operation.

    exports.getAll = (req, res, next) => {
 conn.query("SELECT * FROM tasklist", function (err, data, fields) {
   if(err) return next(new AppError(err))
   res.status(200).json({
     status: "success",
     length: data?.length,
     data: data,
   });
 });
};

    Next, create our createTask handler to add new todos to our database. First, we need to check if the user is sending an empty form before saving the data.

    exports.createTask = (req, res, next) => {
 if (!req.body) return next(new AppError("No form data found", 404));
 const values = [req.body.name, "pending"];
 conn.query(
   "INSERT INTO tasklist (name, status) VALUES(?)",
   [values],
   function (err, data, fields) {
     if (err) return next(new AppError(err, 500));
     res.status(201).json({
       status: "success",
       message: "todo created!",
     });
   }
 );
};

    Then, to get our todos by IDs, we develop a getTask handler. First, we'll see if the id is supplied in the request field, and if it isn't, we'll return an error to the client.

    exports.getTask = (req, res, next) => {
 if (!req.params.id) {
   return next(new AppError("No todo id found", 404));
 }
 conn.query(
   "SELECT * FROM tasklist WHERE id = ?",
   [req.params.id],
   function (err, data, fields) {
     if (err) return next(new AppError(err, 500));
     res.status(200).json({
       status: "success",
       length: data?.length,
       data: data,
     });
   }
 );
};

    Next, to update our todos, we'll write our updateTask handler, which will edit the todo whose id is in the request parameter to be completed.

    exports.updateTask = (req, res, next) => {
 if (!req.params.id) {
   return next(new AppError("No todo id found", 404));
 }
 conn.query(
   "UPDATE tasklist SET status='completed' WHERE id=?",
   [req.params.id],
   function (err, data, fields) {
     if (err) return next(new AppError(err, 500));
     res.status(201).json({
       status: "success",
       message: "todo updated!",
     });
   }
 );
};

    Finally, to delete a todo from our database, we'll create a deleteTask handler. To delete a todo whose id is the request argument, we'll use the delete statement.

    exports.deleteTask = (req, res, next) => {
 if (!req.params.id) {
   return next(new AppError("No todo id found", 404));
 }
 conn.query(
   "DELETE FROM tasklist WHERE id=?",
   [req.params.id],
   function (err, fields) {
     if (err) return next(new AppError(err, 500));
     res.status(201).json({
       status: "success",
       message: "todo deleted!",
     });
   }
 );
}

    Create our Error handlers

    Now that we have all our API controllers setup, let's go ahead and create our error class function with the code snippet below:

    class AppError extends Error {
 constructor(msg, statusCode) {
   super(msg);

   this.statusCode = statusCode;
   this.error = `${statusCode}`.startsWith('4') ? 'fail' : 'error';
   this.isOperational = true;

   Error.captureStackTrace(this, this.constructor);
 }
}
module.exports = AppError;

    Then, without stopping our program, check for probable issues and deliver the associated error and status code to the client.

    module.exports = (err, req, res, next) => {
 err.statusCode = err.statusCode || 500;
 err.status = err.status || "error";
 res.status(err.statusCode).json({
   status: err.status,
   message: err.message,
 });
};

    Create routes

    Now let's create API routes to access our controllers. Create a router.js file and add the code snippets below:

    const express = require("express");
const controllers = require("./controllers");
const router = express.Router();

router.route("/").get(controllers.getAllTodos).post(controllers.createTodo);
router
 .route("/:id")
 .get(controllers.getTodo)
 .put(controllers.updateTodo)
 .delete(controllers.deleteTodo);
module.exports = router;

    Now update the app.js file to import our router with the code snippet below:

    ...
const router = require("./router")
app.use("task/", router);
...

    Conclusion

    Throughout this tutorial, you've learned how to build a REST API in Node.js by creating a todo application. You add more features like authentication, and authorisation to the application you just built.

原文:

Hi Folks, Welcome to another Blog. Today, we'll learn about how to call a rust program using Wasm.
Getting Started:

1. We need rust and wasm-pack in our system, lets install them:

a. For Rust

curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh

b. For wasm-pack

Cargo is a Rust package manager. Cargo downloads your Rust package's dependencies, compiles your packages, makes distributable packages, and uploads them to crates.io, the Rust community's package registry.

2. Create a Library, here I've named it

"ary-kaush"

cargo new --lib ary-kaush

3. Move to the library,

cd ary-kaush/

and open vs code by using code . command.

we can see that there is a folder named src and a file named cargo.toml.

4. In order to create a wasm module, we need to specify the type of package.

For adding such specification we need to modify Cargo.toml file. By adding: a. crate type:

[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib"]

b. dependancies:

5. Change default code in src/lib.rs to the code that we wan't to run

here we are making a function to add two numbers.

6. Now building the package

after build, a lot of files has been created to support the package.

7. In order to run this program, run: 

wasm-pack build --target nodjs

A pkg folder will be created which includes .wasm,.ts, js and .json files.

8. To run our program we need to create a index.js file and where we will make a math library. 

const math  = require('./ary_kaush.js')

console.log(math.add2numbers(10,20));

In VScode

9. move in pkg by cd pkg/ and run

and Finally we have the required output.

This was a basic program in rust, whereas we can create multiple projects e.g. a calculator, fibonacci series, to find whether the number is prime or not etc. .

Please check out blog by @moksh_pathak , where he created a calculator using Rust and ran it in Enarx.

Stay tuned for more stuff!

Write your queries in comment section, we'll help you to resolve your errors.
